name: Browned Onions for Mashed Potatoes
recipeIngredient: 6 large Onions, halved and sliced into half moons
2 tablespoons Dry white wine or vermouth
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the onions, butter, and vegetable stock in a heavy, deep skillet.
2. Cover and bring the mixture to a boil over high heat.
3. Reduce the heat slightly and let it simmer briskly for 10 minutes.
4. Uncover the pan and continue simmering for approximately 35-40 minutes, stirring occasionally until most of the liquid has evaporated.
5. Keep cooking and stirring with constant attention until the onions begin to brown slightly. Use a wooden spatula to scrape up any browned deposits from the bottom of the skillet.
6. Increase the heat to achieve gentle burning (without scorching) on the onions for a few minutes, then add the white wine or vermouth and continue boiling it until just evaporated while stirring vigorously and scraping up the browned bits.
7. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
8. Remove from the heat and serve immediately over mashed potatoes. Store covered in the refrigerator for several days.
